style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;rounded=0;" value="Special In content, in course material(s) case: Assume a student asks a question that has 2 parts, 1 in content and the other either in the course material or even outside it. Let us take the following example: "What is the difference between Quick Sort and a new sorting algorithm released in a recent paper?" While the quick sort is part of the curriculum and the course material, the other algorithm is not within our memory/database.<br><br><div>In this scenario, we can use cosine similarity, sort in ascending order, and use the least similiar word as the decider for which path to take in our decision-making.<br><br>so the agent won't answer the question and apologize</div>" vertex="1"> data will be collected:</h1><blockquote style="margin: 0 0 0 40px; border: none; padding: 0px;"><div><font style="font-size: 19px;">1- multi-modal emotional analysis with late fusion&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 19px;">2-pptagent training on data collected from the internet&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 19px;">3- Lip syncing + facial expression 3d avatar data collection and training&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 19px;">4-intent classification, training on pairs example: &lt;student said: "x", this means: "y"&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;</font></div><div><font style="font-size: 19px;">5-Assessments (code and MCQ) &amp; placement quiz generation</font></div></blockquote>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="300" div.div:
Special In content, in course material(s) case: Assume a student asks a question that has 2 parts, 1 in content and the other either in the course material or even outside it. Let us take the following example: "What is the difference between Quick Sort and a new sorting algorithm released in a recent paper?" While the quick sort is part of the curriculum and the course material, the other algorithm is not within our memory/database.
In this scenario, we can use cosine similarity, sort in ascending order, and use the least similiar word as the decider for which path to take in our decision-making.
so the agent won't answer the question and apologize

div.h1: How do we measure how much a student understands a topic? And how should we quantify it?
div.p: Assume we have a student answering a question he answered correctly. Do we change it as a percentage or a rating from 1 to 10, or are there other ways to quantify how much a student understands a topic, like a 0,1 Binary Understanding.

div.h1: Models that will be trained and data will be collected:

div.font: 1- multi-modal emotional analysis with late fusion
2-pptagent training on data collected from the internet
3- Lip syncing + facial expression 3d avatar data collection and training
4-intent classification, training on pairs example: <student said: "x", this means: "y">
